segments.text: Am I the asshole for refusing to attend my sister's vegan wedding?
Alright, so here's the thing.
My sister, Anna, recently decided she wanted to have an all-vegan wedding.
No meat, no dairy, nothing that involves animals in any way.
She's been vegan for a while now, and it's pretty important to her lifestyle.
Cool, I respect that.
I'm a chef, I love and respect all kinds of food, and I've even cooked up some vegan dishes in my time.
But here's where things got messy.
My dad, a carnivore through and through, wasn't happy with this idea.
He's old fashioned and the concept of a wedding without some sort of meat, as the main course was just unthinkable to him.
He was vocal about it, and that led to some big fights between him and Anna.
I mostly stayed out of it.
Then, out of nowhere, Anna decided that it wasn't enough for the wedding food to be vegan.
But all the guests had to be too, at least for the day of the wedding.
As in, no meat, dairy, or animal products would be allowed to be consumed by anyone attending, even at their own homes before the wedding.
Now, let me clarify.
I love my sister, but I don't think it's fair to impose your lifestyle on someone else for a whole day.
It's not like she's allergic to meat or anything.
It's just a lifestyle choice, her choice, not everyone else's.
And it created quite a shitstorm in our family.
A few of our relatives have dietary restrictions where they need certain nutrients, and it led to a lot of drama.
Seeing as I wasn't keen on getting involved, I voiced that I thought this was somewhat extreme, but said I will adhere to the rules for her big day.
She wasn't happy with this and said, I have to be 100% into or not come at all.
It's about supporting her lifestyle.
With that kind of ultimatum, I felt like I was being punished for questioning her rule.
Here's the thing, I was going to just roll with it, you know, but her all or nothing attitude kind of ticked me off.
So I told her I wouldn't be attending the wedding if this how she's gonna be about it.
She was pretty upset and our parents are now furious with me.
Look, I'm not trying to disrespect her lifestyle.
It's just annoying that she thinks it's cool to dictate how everyone else lives their life for a day.
I was willing to be vegan for the duration of her wedding out of respect.
But her expectations seem overly demanding and not at all in the wedding spirit.
I don't feel I should be shut out for speaking my mind.
So, Reddit folks, I'm seeking validation or an ego check here.
Am I the asshole for refusing to attend my sister's vegan wedding?
